Identity.Name: StingWeldTypes
Identity.Definition: Weld type codes per AWS A2.4 / BS EN ISO 2553. Drives fabrication spool drawings, NC1 export records, weld-map exports, NDT regime selection, and the COBie fabrication register.

Governance.StandardsBasis: AWS A2.4-2020 (US); BS EN ISO 2553:2019 (UK / EU); ASME IX

Value.Definition: Butt Weld — full-penetration end-to-end weld between two pipe / plate edges.
Fillet Weld — triangular cross-section at a T or lap joint.
Socket Weld — pipe inserted into socket fitting, then fillet-welded around perimeter.
Lap Weld — two overlapping plates joined with fillet welds on each side.
Tack Weld — temporary short-bead weld to hold assembly during full welding.
Spot Weld — resistance-welded contact point; sheet-metal duct seams.
Seam Weld — continuous resistance / fusion weld along seam length.
Plug Weld — weld through a circular hole in one part to fuse to the part beneath.
Projection Weld — resistance weld at engineered raised projections.
Flanged joint (no weld) — bolted flange, included here for spool-drawing consistency.
